commit.message:
Add reverse-engineered SAP decision algorithm analysis with sourced data (#13)

* Add reverse-engineered SAP decision algorithm analysis

Work backward from Scenario 17 outcomes to reconstruct the likely
methodology: test 5 hypotheses (utilization threshold, capacity
threshold, cascade model, adequacy-weighted, proximity-driven)
against the closure cohort data. Conclude the SAP is primarily a
building-selection algorithm driven by proximity + adequacy + capacity,
with utilization carrying near-zero effective weight.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ANcYNzVtsqLLMhhhYXpBjX

* Source the reverse-engineered SAP methodology with HPM adequacy data

Major findings from sourced DCSD/HPM Educational Adequacy Reports:
- APES adequacy score is 70.0% (confirmed from official HPM report)
- Downloaded 13 school adequacy reports from DCSD website
- Adequacy does NOT predict closure: Livsey ES (50.6%) gets EXPAND
  while APES (70.0%) gets CLOSE
- Site acreage is a stronger predictor: APES has smallest site (7.02
  acres, 62.5% of 11.24-acre target) of any sourced school
- Built expandability formula from sourced data that correctly predicts
  Chamblee cluster outcomes
- All claims now traced to specific DCSD/HPM documents with URLs

New files:
- data/processed/educational_adequacy.csv (13 schools, all sourced)
- data/raw/externally_sourced/adequacy_reports/ (13 HPM PDFs)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ANcYNzVtsqLLMhhhYXpBjX

fix(ci): use goinstall mode for golangci-lint to support Go 1.26

the linter from source using the runner's Go 1.26 toolchain.

test(acceptance): check モードのバイナリ受入テスト追加 (issue #48) (#49)

* test(acceptance): add check mode binary-level tests with fake DNS/IP (issue #48)

Add DIPPER_AI_FAKE_IP_V4/V6 env vars to ip.Fetch and
DIPPER_AI_FAKE_DNS env var to check mode to allow injecting
controlled values in acceptance tests without real network.

New acceptance tests:
- TestBinary_Check_AllMatch_SilentExit: DNS matches → exit 0, no output
- TestBinary_Check_Mismatch_TriggersUpdate: stale DNS → update fires
  (verified via local httptest.Server standing in for MyDNS endpoint)
- TestBinary_Check_PartialMismatch_OnlyStaleUpdated: 2 domains, only
  the stale one gets updated

Also refactors runBinary to runBinaryEnv for extra env var support.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(acceptance): pre-seed ok-domain cache and set UPDATE_TIME=1440

TestBinary_Check_PartialMismatch_OnlyStaleUpdated failed because:
1. UPDATE_TIME=1 (writeConf default) caused forceSync=true → all MyDNS
   entries were force-updated regardless of per-domain cache.
2. Empty stateDir caused ReadDomainCache to return "0.0.0.0" for the
   ok domain, making Update() treat it as an IP change.

Fix: set UPDATE_TIME=1440 in the test config and pre-seed
cache_mydns_0_ipv4 with the current IP so Update() skips it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(acceptance): pre-touch gate_update to prevent forceSync in partial mismatch test

ShouldRun() returns true when the gate file does not exist, regardless of
UPDATE_TIME. This caused forceSync=true and forced all MyDNS entries to be
updated, breaking TestBinary_Check_PartialMismatch_OnlyStaleUpdated.

Pre-create gate_update with the current timestamp so the 1440-minute
UPDATE_TIME gate correctly reports ShouldRun()=false.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix memory allocation in BoundaryMergeGPU merge

CRITICAL FIX:
- SVD produces new bond dimension k != chi_bond
- Was trying to write k elements into chi_bond-sized buffer
- Now uses temporary buffers, then reallocates boundary data correctly

Changes:
- Allocate temp buffers for A_left, A_right, V with max_bond size
- Perform SVD split into temp buffers
- Reallocate left->d_psi_left, right->d_psi_right, left->d_V with k_out size
- Copy from temp buffers to reallocated memory
- Free temp buffers

This ensures boundary data always has correct size for new bond dimension.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Remove exclusive memory slot to coexist with built-in memory

Cortex was declaring kind: "memory" which caused OpenClaw to replace
the default memory plugin entirely. This meant the agent lost access
to file-writing tools (USER.md, daily logs) when cortex was installed.

Drop the kind declaration so cortex supplements the built-in memory
system instead of replacing it. Both plugins now fire their hooks
additively — the default handles local files while cortex handles
long-term API-based recall and capture.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: openclaw-cortex
repository.description: OpenClaw plugin for Cortex — automatic long-term memory with knowledge graph retrieval. Auto-recall injects relevant memories before every agent turn, auto-capture extracts facts after responses, and file sync ingests MEMORY.md and daily logs in the background.

commit.message:
fix(party): use admin client for soft-delete to bypass RLS

The UPDATE RLS policy's USING clause (deleted_at IS NULL) can block
soft-delete updates via the authenticated client. Use admin client
with ownership check instead, matching the restoreParty pattern.

Warm up audio tone to sound like acoustic guitar

Replace raw white noise excitation with low-pass shaped burst, increase

(200Hz boost, 2.2kHz dip, 3.5kHz lowpass) and soft attack envelope.

commit.message:
Fix 2 critical bugs: double-defeat race condition + timer desync

Race condition — both players see "Defeat":
- game-over page retries fetch up to 3x (2s delay) when result is
  null, shows "Result is being processed..." as fallback
- move/route.ts timeout path now broadcasts game_update to both
  clients (was missing — only checkmate/draw paths had it)
- game_update handler always accepts server FINISHED result, even
  if client already set gameOver locally (prevents mismatch)

Timer 25s drift between players:
- new_move broadcast handler now syncs the mover's timer with
  server-authoritative moveData.time_remaining on every opponent
  move, preventing cumulative local countdown drift

Update CLAUDE.md with all fixed bugs status.

Add real-time Tofler enrichment via proxy, manual verification, and CIN decoder

- Add /api/enrich endpoint using codetabs proxy to fetch live Tofler data
- Add /api/company/enrich endpoint to update company DB with enriched data
- Add ComplianceOverride model for persistent manual verification
- Add PUT /api/compliance handler for marking checks as verified
- Dashboard: auto-enriches company data on add, manual verify UI
- mca-service: CIN decoder fallback, last filing dates in checks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add missing tests: requestinterception-experimental spec (#3313) (#3350)

* Add missing tests: requestinterception-experimental spec (#3313)

Port 5 missing tests from upstream requestinterception-experimental.spec:
- should be able to access the error reason (Page.setRequestInterception)
- should be able to access the response (Request.respond)
- should indicate already-handled if an intercept has been handled (Request.respond)
- should work for document type (Request.resourceType)
- should work for stylesheets (Request.resourceType)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix request interception tests that depend on ConcurrentSet ordering

The ShouldBeAbleToAccessTheErrorReason and ShouldBeAbleToAccessTheResponse
tests used two separate AddRequestInterceptor calls where the second
interceptor read state set by the first. Since interceptors are stored in a
ConcurrentSet (backed by ConcurrentDictionary), enumeration order is not
guaranteed. This caused the second interceptor to sometimes run before the
first, reading default/null values. Fixed by combining both operations into
a single interceptor and adding BiDi request support alongside CDP.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add per-state concurrency limits (max_concurrent_by_state) (#204)

Add max_concurrent_by_state config option that allows limiting concurrent
sessions by session state (e.g. max 5 running, max 2 pr_open). State
names are normalized (trim + lowercase) for comparison. Falls back to
global max_parallel when no per-state override is defined.

The running state limit is enforced during worker dispatch in the
orchestrator. Per-state limits and current counts are displayed in
`maestro status` output.

Implements #199

Split OOD exercises into individual files per exercise

exercises/ood-system-design.json is now a lean index containing only
metadata (id, title, file path, classCount, patternCount) for each
of the 21 exercises.

Full exercise data lives in exercises/ood/{id}-{slug}.json — one file
per exercise. This keeps each file compact and self-contained.

loadExercise() is now async and lazy-loads individual files on first
access, caching them in state.exercises for subsequent use. The card
grid in the exercise selection view is unchanged (uses index counts).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Windows API focus management for external input mode

Root cause: MiniWindow had OS focus when Ctrl+V was sent, so paste went
to Whisper's own text area instead of the external app.

Fix: 5-layer defense against focus stealing:
- WS_EX_NOACTIVATE via ctypes on MiniWindow to prevent activation
- WA_ShowWithoutActivating Qt attribute on MiniWindow
- GetForegroundWindow + SetForegroundWindow with AttachThreadInput
  and ALT key fallback to restore focus to target app before Ctrl+V
- Conditional ensureCursorVisible() - skipped in external mode
- Reordered operations: external paste before internal append

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
